response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong web/backend breadth, but the role demands 8 years of C++ and 5 years of large-scale distributed/cloud-compute infrastructure. C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and there is no evidence of cloud compute platform leadership, distributed systems at scale, or formal technical leadership of cloud/DevOps engineers. Background skews toward TypeScript/Node/DLang product work rather than systems-level infrastructure.
strengths: Long polyglot career with mentoring, workshops, and OSS leadership (GISCollective, Trial, Fluent-Asserts)
Exposure to high-throughput systems via Dunnhumby/Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of req/s
Strong craftsmanship discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, Docker, Kubernetes deployments
concerns: Effectively no recent C++ experience; falls drastically short of the 8-year C++ minimum
No demonstrated cloud compute platform architecture or 5+ years building large-scale distributed infrastructure
No formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers; current career gap and short recent tenures

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform / distributed infra leadership
Breadth of engineering craftsmanship and OSS/mentoring
